You have now developed forty images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at twenty-two noon and eighteen golden hour. The noon conversion rate continues to hold: fourteen of twenty-two noon images score 2 or above, against eight of eighteen for golden hour. The three most recent additions — "Every Exit, Unreachable," "After the Emptying," and "Still Breathing, Still Music" — score 1, 1, and 2 respectively, with the two 1s both golden-hour and the 2 noon, consistent with the pattern.

The bougainvillea has appeared in nineteen images and remains dormant by instruction; its last three appearances — "Still Life After Subtraction," "After the Heat Stayed," and "Still Breathing, Still Music" — were decorative rather than structural, the red bloom functioning as elegy-accent rather than friction-generator, and it should not return until it can produce resistance rather than warmth. The jacaranda sits at ten appearances; "Every Exit, Unreachable" did not use it, but the reflex toward it in defiance and vigil registers remains strong enough to require active suppression. The handprint accident in "Every Exit, Unreachable" — children's mural hands above tangled metal below, innocence-marker collapsed into barrier — is the sharpest single-object collision since the gate-shadow in "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" and the feather's barring-as-fingerprint in "Blank Authority"; none of these three has been followed up, and all three point toward surface-as-record as a device that has been touched but not developed. The gesture-driven strategy — palm to map, two hands on one screen — has not recurred across the last eight images and remains the most underexplored compositional argument available to you.

Among registers, aftermath leads at fourteen, vigil at eight, endurance at eight, suspension at five, defiance at five, and menace at three, with fury and interiority still completely untouched. Trace scores over the last ten images run 1, 2, 2, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2 — the floor holds but the 2s are less frequent than the previous run, the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" is now twenty-two images back and still the unreached ceiling, and the conditions that produced it remain only partially reassembled: non-figurative focal object, setting working against the register, accident carrying structural rather than decorative weight.

The body of work has a reliable grammar and a stable floor, but its range has narrowed slightly in the last ten images and the next ceiling requires a non-figurative image in a setting that actively contradicts its register.
